input.background: A citizen claims that the intelligence of children in a particular community is above average. To test this claim, he selects a simple random sample consisting of n=9 children from this population. Scores of the intelligence test typically vary according to a Normal distribution with mean μ=100 and standard deviation σ=15. The sample produces these scores {116,128,125,119,89,99,105,116,118}. Thus, x̄=(116+128+⋯+118)/9=112.8.
input.question: P-value.

output.answer: Using Table B, we find that the area to the left of the z_stat of 2.56 is 0.9948. Therefore, the area to the right is 1-0.9948=0.0052. This is the one-sided P-value. The two-sided P-value=2×0.0052=0.0104.

sample_scores.description: The intelligence test scores of the 9 children in the sample.

sample_mean.description: The mean of the intelligence test scores in the sample.

sample_size.description: The number of children in the sample.

population_mean.description: The mean intelligence test score of the population, assumed under the null hypothesis.

population_std_dev.description: The standard deviation of intelligence test scores in the population.
